content.full_text:
September 7, 1976

Mr. Robert Naylor  
Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro  
Post Office Box 7880  
San Francisco, California 94120  

Dear Mr. Naylor:

This letter is to confirm my advice to you about the reporting of cumulative contributions by your client, Standard Oil Company.

I advised that all contributions to a single candidate or committee must be combined for a period beginning with the closing date of the last post-election statement filed and ending with the filing of the next post-election statement. If a person has not filed a post-election statement since the effective date of the Political Reform Act, the period begins January 7, 1975. Therefore, after filing the post-election statement for the 1976 primary election, Standard Oil should begin cumulating contributions anew for purposes of reporting in connection with the November general election.

I hope this information clarifies your client's campaign disclosure filing requirements.

Sincerely,

Nan Hambleton  
Special Compliance Representative

NH:bw

bcc: Jeanne P.

sections.question_synthetic: How should cumulative contributions by Standard Oil Company to candidates or committees be reported for campaign disclosure purposes?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: All contributions to a single candidate or committee must be combined for the reporting period between post-election statements. After filing the post-election statement for the 1976 primary election, Standard Oil should begin cumulating contributions anew for the November general election reporting.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections; letter is written as confirmation of prior verbal advice. Key information extracted from body paragraphs.

After filing the post-election statement for the 1976 primary election, Standard Oil should begin cumulating contributions anew for the November general election reporting.
